markets.question: Exact Score: Real Betis Balompié 0 - 0 Elche CF?
markets.description: In the upcoming La Liga game between Real Betis Balompié and Elche CF, scheduled for May 12, 2026 at 2:00 PM ET: This market will resolve based on the final score of the Real Betis Balompié vs. Elche CF match originally scheduled for May 12, 2026 at 2:00 PM ET, considering only the result at the end of 90 minutes of regulation plus stoppage time; extra time and penalty shoot-outs are excluded. If the actual score is not one of the explicitly listed outcomes, the market resolves to "Any Other Score." If the match is postponed, the market will remain open until it is completed; if it is canceled with no make-up game, the market resolves to "0-0." The primary resolution source for this market will be the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if final official statistics are not published within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Real Betis hosts Elche CF in La Liga round 36 at Estadio Benito Villamarín, with the Verdiblancos in 5th place chasing European spots via solid home form, while Elche sits 14th in mid-table safety. Elche's recent momentum from April victories over Atlético Madrid (3-2) and Valencia (1-0) has boosted trader sentiment for a competitive outing, mirroring their earlier 1-1 draw against Betis this season. Betis contends with key absences including Marc Bartra (foot injury) and Ángel Ortiz (muscle), potentially weakening defense, alongside Junior Firpo doubts; Elche misses Adam Boayar (muscle) and Yago Santiago. Expect a tight matchup influenced by home advantage, recent clean sheets, and late-season stakes.

markets.question: Will 50-74 ships transit the Strait of Hormuz between April 27-May 3?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the total number of transit calls that IMF Portwatch reports for the Strait of Hormuz for all days from April 27, 2026, through May 3, 2026, inclusive.

Transit calls include container, dry bulk, roll-on/roll-off, general cargo, and tanker ships. Ships not reported by IMF Portwatch will not be considered.

This market will resolve as soon as data has been published for the final date in the specified period. If no data has been published for the final date of the specified period within 14 calendar days (ET) after the end of that period, this market will resolve based on data published up to that point.

Revisions to previously published data points made within this market’s timeframe will be considered. Revisions to previously published data points after data is published for the final date of the specified period, however, will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be IMF Portwatch, specifically the transit calls data published for the Strait of Hormuz at https://portwatch.imf.org/pages/cb5856222a5b4105adc6ee7e880a1730, both in the chart and through downloadable files.

series.title: ships transit the Strait of Hormuz

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket overwhelmingly prices a 97.9% implied probability for 25-49 ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz during April 27-May 3, 2026, reflecting verified automatic identification system (AIS) data from sources like Windward.ai and IMF Portwatch amid the ongoing 2026 Strait of Hormuz crisis. Dual naval blockades—Iran's since late February and the U.S. imposition in mid-April—have slashed daily transits to 5-20 vessels, mostly Iran-linked tankers, down over 90% from pre-conflict norms of 125-140 crossings per day, as commercial operators avoid heightened geopolitical risks, elevated war-risk insurance premiums, and supply chain disruptions. Sporadic passages, such as the Idemitsu Maru tanker on April 28, underscore the trickle. A realistic challenge would require an abrupt ceasefire or U.S.-Iran de-escalation to spur a traffic surge, though no such signals have emerged ahead of final Portwatch data publication.

markets.question: Map 2: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 2 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 2 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 2, or if Map 2 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 2 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 2 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 2 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Counter-Strike: Imperial Academy vs Infinite (BO3) - United21 Playoffs
events.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Upper bracket quarterfinal 1 match between Imperial Academy and Infinite in the United21 Playoffs, initially scheduled for April 9 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Imperial Academy" if Imperial Academy win the match against Infinite.

This market will resolve to "Infinite" if Infinite win the match against Imperial Academy.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Imperial Academy entered the United21 Season 47 playoffs upper bracket quarterfinal as undefeated Group A winners, posting a 2-0 record with +16 round differential through dominant wins over LPH Gaming (2-1) and Sangal ALTERS (2-0), highlighting strong map control on Inferno, Dust II, and Ancient. Infinite advanced as shaky Group D runners-up (2-1, -9 rounds), rebounding from a 0-2 loss to Misa Esports via a decider over AaB esport but leaning on substitutes like SHiPZ and marTineZ amid roster flux. HLTV #193 Imperial Academy's young core (average age 18.9, ratings ~1.10) edged Infinite's lower-tier lineup in recent European Pro League form, shaping trader consensus for this double-elimination BO3 in the $10K online European C-Tier event.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors the United Kingdom at 34% implied probability for last place in Eurovision 2026's grand final, closely trailed by host nation Austria at 27.5%, reflecting backlash from first rehearsal clips released in the past 48 hours. The UK's experimental entry "Eins, Zwei, Drei" by YouTuber Look Mum No Computer—featuring eccentric synth machines—has sparked social media mockery and fears of nul points from televoters, echoing the Big Five nation's dismal recent history. Austria's "Tanzschein" by COSMÓ, selected via national final, draws similar criticism as bland and unmemorable despite host-country buzz, with vocal coaches predicting bottom finishes. Key differentiators include UK's polarizing novelty versus Austria's safer but lackluster staging; semi-final qualifiers and jury-televote splits through May 16 could shift dynamics amid fierce competition from Finland and Greece frontrunners.

markets.question: Will Jorge Rodríguez be the de facto leader of Venezuela at the end of 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the individual who de facto holds and exercises the powers of the head of state of Venezuela on December 31, 2026 at 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, “de facto holds” refers to the individual who exercises primary executive authority over the Venezuelan state at that time, including effective control over the armed forces, national institutions, and core executive decision-making, regardless of formal title, constitutional designation, or international recognition.

Formal appointment, recognition by the United Nations, or recognition by foreign governments is not required.

If more than one individual claims to be head of state, this market will resolve to the individual who demonstrably exercises primary governing control within Venezuela’s territory at the specified time.

If no individual exercises effective governing control at the specified time, this market will resolve to “No Head of State”.

Indicators of de facto authority may include control over the armed forces and security services; control over executive ministries and state administrative institutions; enforcement of national laws; issuance of binding national directives; and effective control over the capital and core state infrastructure.

Symbolic status, foreign recognition without domestic control, nomination without effective authority, or expired prior service will not qualify.

The primary resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting assessing who exercises effective governing authority at the specified time.

markets.question: Will the Bank of Israel decrease the Bank of Israel Interest Rate after the May decision?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the change in the Bank of Israel Interest Rate resulting from the Bank of Israel’s May monetary policy decision, relative to the level it was prior to this decision.

The resolution source for this market is information released by the Bank of Israel after its May 25, 2026 monetary policy decision, as listed on the official Bank of Israel interest rate decision schedule: https://www.boi.org.il/en/economic-roles/monetary-policy/interest-rate-announcement-dates-2025-2026/#

This market may resolve as soon as the Bank of Israel's announcement of their May 25, 2026 decision with relevant data is issued. If no decision on the Bank of Israel Interest Rate is issued by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders price a 73% implied probability for a Bank of Israel interest rate decrease at the upcoming May 25-28 meeting, reflecting trader consensus on easing amid March 2026 CPI inflation easing to 1.9% annually—below the 2% target midpoint—and the shekel's surge to 33-year highs against the USD, now near 2.90, which curbs imported inflation but pressures exporters. The benchmark rate has held steady at 4.00% since January's 25 basis point cut, with March's no-change decision citing Iran conflict risks despite governor hints at further reductions. Sluggish growth forecasts around 3.8% for 2026 reinforce cut expectations, though geopolitical uncertainty supports the 26.5% no-change odds, while hikes remain negligible at 0.4%.

markets.question: Will Paloma Valencia place 2nd in the 1st round of the 2026 Colombian presidential election?
markets.description:
Colombia's presidential elections are scheduled for May 31, 2026, and a second round (if required) on June 21, 2026, in case no candidate secures more than 50% of the valid votes in the first round.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate who receives the second-most valid votes in the first round of this election.

The named candidates will be primarily ranked by the number of valid votes received in the specified election. If two or more candidates are tied on valid votes, ties will be broken by alphabetical order of the candidates' last names. This market will resolve to the candidate that occupies the second-highest finishing position after applying this ranking.

If the results of the first round of the Colombian presidential election are not known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the election results, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by Colombia's National Civil Registry (Registraduría Nacional del Estado Civil) (https://registraduria.gov.co).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors Abelardo de la Espriella to finish second in Colombia's May 31 first-round presidential vote, reflecting recent polling trends where he trails frontrunner Iván Cepeda but leads Paloma Valencia. AtlasIntel's late-April survey showed Cepeda at 38%, de la Espriella at 30%, and Valencia at 21%, while Yanhaas trackers indicate a tighter race with Valencia recently at 29% to de la Espriella's 19-23% averages. De la Espriella's outsider appeal and strong simulated runoff margins against Cepeda—often 48-50% to 41%—bolster his position amid right-wing fragmentation post-March primaries, where Valencia won the Democratic Center nod. Cepeda's consolidated left support limits his second-place odds, with undecided voters and no major consolidation as key wild cards ahead of the ballot.

Lower odds for Anthony Edwards and others signal trader expectations of those teams' eliminations.

markets.question: Will Meituan have the best Coding AI model at the end of May 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the highest arena rank based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab for "Coding" is checked on May 31, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Coding" Leaderboard tab at https://arena.ai/leaderboard/text/coding-no-style-control with style control off will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie still remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies first place under this ranking.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Wuhan be 31°C on May 11?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Wuhan Tianhe International Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 11 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Wuhan Tianhe International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/cn/wuhan/ZHHH.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

markets.question: Will the Fed decrease interest rates by 50+ bps after the September 2026 meeting?
markets.description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal funds range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to the amount of basis points the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is changed by versus the level it was prior to the Federal Reserve's September 2026 meeting.

If the target federal funds rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded up to the nearest 25 and will resolve to the relevant bracket. (e.g. if there's a cut/increase of 12.5 bps it will be considered to be 25 bps)

The resolution source for this market is the FOMC’s statement after its meeting scheduled for September 15-16, 2026 according to the official calendar: https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/fomccalendars.htm.

The level and change of the target federal funds rate is also published at the official website of the Federal Reserve at https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the FOMC’s statement for their September meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed individual is included on the official candidate list for the first round of the 2027 French presidential election. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A record number of candidates have declared—including Édouard Philippe (Horizons), Republicans like Bruno Retailleau and Xavier Bertrand, Socialists Ségolène Royal and Jérôme Guedj, and independents like Nicolas Dupont-Aignan—while polls show National Rally's Jordan Bardella leading first-round voting at 35-38%. Marine Le Pen remains ineligible pending a July 7 appeal on her embezzlement conviction ban, potentially paving Bardella's path. Ballot access requires 500 parrainages from elected officials, with a United Left primary set for October 11.

markets.question: T20 Hong Kong Tri-Series, Women: Hong Kong, China vs Malaysia - Who wins the toss?
markets.description:
This market refers to the pre-match coin toss for the cricket match between Hong Kong, China and Malaysia scheduled for 2026-05-10 in T20 Hong Kong Tri-Series, Women.
This market resolves according to the official coin toss result (the team recorded as having won the toss) as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
If an official toss winner is recorded, the market will resolve based on that result even if the match is subsequently delayed, relocated, abandoned, or no ball is bowled.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market will remain open and resolve according to the toss result for the listed fixture when it is eventually played.
If the fixture is permanently canceled, forfeited before any toss is conducted, or otherwise completed without an official toss winner being recorded, the market will resolve 50-50.

events.title: T20 Hong Kong Tri-Series, Women: Hong Kong, China vs Malaysia
events.description:

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

markets.question: Will Letitia James be arrested before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed individual is arrested by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A qualifying arrest/detention includes:
* Being taken into physical custody by law enforcement authorities (including federal, state, local, military, or international law enforcement)
* Voluntarily surrendering to law enforcement authorities in response to an arrest warrant
* Being formally booked or processed following detention
* Being handcuffed by a state authority and taken to a police station
* Surrendering at a police station or courthouse with their attorney
* Being placed under house arrest or electronic monitoring

The following scenarios will NOT qualify as an arrest/detention
* An arrest warrant being issued but not executed
* Being briefly detained but not arrested (e.g. such as in the South Korean judicial system when an individual is awaiting a judge’s decision on whether to grant a detention warrant)
* Being questioned or interviewed without arrest
* Being named in an indictment without arrest

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the arresting government and law enforcement, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: LoL: Invictus Gaming vs Bilibili Gaming (BO3) - LPL Group Ascend
markets.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Invictus Gaming and Bilibili Gaming in the LPL Group Ascend, initially scheduled for May 12 at 7:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Invictus Gaming" if Invictus Gaming win the match against Bilibili Gaming.

This market will resolve to "Bilibili Gaming" if Bilibili Gaming win the match against Invictus Gaming.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Will Trump say "Turkey" or "Turkiye" in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Donald Trump mentions the listed term between May 1, 2026, 12:00 AM ET and May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Plural and possessive forms of the listed term will count toward the resolution of this market regardless of context; however, other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

A ‘mention’ will include any verbal mention which is recorded (audio or video) and publicly accessible.

Written usages of terms (e.g. Truth Social posts, written statements, etc.) will not count toward this market's resolution.

AI-generated audio or video will not count toward this market's resolution.

Videos posted on his social media which were filmed outside this market's time frame will not qualify for a "Yes" resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rep. Mike Collins leads trader consensus at 67% implied probability to win Georgia's Republican U.S. Senate primary on May 19, driven by consistent polling edges in recent Quantus Insights surveys showing him at 33% versus Derek Dooley's 23% amid 29% undecideds, bolstered by Club for Growth backing, strong fundraising nearing $2 million, and his House record on bills like the Laken Riley Act. Dooley's 30% odds reflect Gov. Brian Kemp's endorsement, a polling surge, and outsider appeal as a former coach, though heated debates with Collins and Rep. Buddy Carter (now at 2%) have highlighted infighting. Early voting underway keeps the race fluid, with no candidate near the 50% runoff threshold.

markets.question: Will the Democratic Party win the NY-07 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the NY-07 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Longtime Rep. Nydia Velázquez's November 2025 retirement opened NY-07—a progressive stronghold in Brooklyn, Queens, and Lower Manhattan—but trader consensus reflects 93.5% implied probability for a Democratic general election win on November 3, 2026, driven by the district's heavy partisan lean and absence of credible Republican challengers or fundraising. Recent primary jockeying, including Brooklyn Borough President Antonio Reynoso's March endorsement from Rep. Pat Ryan and April reports of intra-left proxy battles among candidates like Councilwoman Julie Won, underscores the June 23 Democratic primary as the contest's decider, with no polling indicating GOP viability. Upsets would require a high-profile Republican recruit, nominee scandal, or national midterm wave, though historical base rates in safe Democratic seats make such shifts improbable.

markets.question: Internazionali BNL d'Italia: Elina Svitolina vs Nikola Bartunkova
markets.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Elina Svitolina and Nikola Bartunkova in the Internazionali BNL d'Italia, originally scheduled for May 11, 2026 at 5:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Elina Svitolina' if Elina Svitolina advances against Nikola Bartunkova.

This market will resolve to 'Nikola Bartunkova' if Nikola Bartunkova advances against Elina Svitolina.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the WTA Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Two-time Internazionali BNL d'Italia champion Elina Svitolina, currently ranked inside the WTA top 10, cruised past Hailey Baptiste in straight sets to reach the round of 16, building on her proven clay-court prowess at Rome with a 5-2 record on the surface this year. She meets world No. 94 Nikola Bartunkova, whose breakout run as a lucky loser continued with a 6-3, 1-6, 6-4 upset over Madison Keys—the first such player to advance this far since 2013—fueled by recent ITF titles and a daring style. No head-to-head exists, leaving trader consensus to balance Svitolina's experience against Bartunkova's momentum on outdoor clay, with no confirmed injuries despite Svitolina's medical timeout in Madrid last month.

markets.question: Will Seattle have between 0.5 and 1 inches of precipitation in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the total precipitation in inches in Seattle between May 1 and May 31, 2026, 11:59PM ET according to the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA).

The resolution source for this market will be NOAA, specifically the figure for May 2026 when the "Monthly summarized data" for "Seattle City Area" is selected with the variable set to "Precipitation" at the https://www.weather.gov/wrh/climate?wfo=sew link once that figure is finalized for the whole month of May 2026.

If the reported data falls exactly between two brackets, this market will resolve to the higher bracket.

The resolution source for this market measures precipitation to 2 decimal places (e.g., 1.54), thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving this market. Any revisions to precipitation recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

markets.question: Will gas hit (High) $4.60 by May 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if on any day between market creation and May 31, 2026, the average US regular gas price is equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

Only the first two decimal digits of the reported price will be considered (e.g., if the price is reported as $3.257, this market will use $3.25 as the price).

The resolution source for this market will be information from the American Automobile Association (AAA), presently found here: https://gasprices.aaa.com/. Specifically, the cell under "Regular" and for the row "Current Avg".

eventMetadata.context_description: U.S. national average regular gasoline prices have surged to $4.52 per gallon as of May 10, propelled by Brent crude topping $101/bbl amid Strait of Hormuz disruptions from escalating Middle East tensions and domestic refinery outages, which have constrained supply ahead of peak summer driving demand. EIA's latest Weekly Petroleum Status Report for the week ending May 1 showed crude inventories down 2.3 million barrels, with refinery inputs steady at 16 million bpd, while gasoline futures (RBOB) jumped over 2% recently. Trader consensus reflects heightened volatility, with Memorial Day (May 25) poised to accelerate consumption; the next EIA report on May 13 will provide critical inventory updates ahead of May 31 resolution.

markets.question: Will the Republicans win the 2028 US Presidential Election?
markets.description:
The 2028 United States presidential election will be held on Tuesday, November 7, 2028.

This market will resolve to the political party whose candidate is elected the next President of the United States in the 2028 election.

The resolution source for this market is the Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC. This market will resolve once all three sources call the race for the same party. If all three sources haven’t called the race for the same party by the inauguration date (January 20, 2029) this market will resolve based on who is inaugurated.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices Democrats at a 60.5% implied probability to win the 2028 presidential election, driven by President Trump's approval ratings sinking to the low 30s amid inflation, high gas prices, and escalating military tensions with Iran, as shown in May polls. These factors fuel heavy expectations of Democratic gains in the November 2026 midterms—markets give over 80% odds on Democrats retaking the House and 44% for a full sweep—potentially building momentum into the open-seat race after Trump's term limit. Early nominee markets favor Gavin Newsom (25%) for Democrats over JD Vance (37%) for Republicans, though historical midterm losses for the president's party average 28 House seats, leaving outcomes uncertain ahead of primaries and generic ballot shifts.

markets.question: Abstract FDV above $200M one day after launch?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of Abstract's governance token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

The FDV will be determined using the total token supply multiplied by the token price.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Abstract doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

eventMetadata.context_description: Carlos Alcaraz's withdrawal from the 2026 French Open due to a lingering right wrist injury—announced April 24 after forcing him out of Madrid and Rome—has propelled Jannik Sinner to a dominant 70% implied probability as the world No. 1 and top seed on clay at Roland Garros. Sinner's trader consensus reflects his five straight Masters 1000 titles, including strong clay showings like Madrid, superior clay performance score (92.0), and career Grand Slam pursuit, leaving little competition with the back-to-back champion absent. Alexander Zverev's 7% follows his projected No. 2 seeding and consistent Roland Garros deep runs, while Rafael Nadal's protégé Rafael Jodar (4.2%), a rising 19-year-old Spaniard at career-high No. 34 with recent Challenger wins, and Novak Djokovic (3.9%) trail amid form questions and age, underscoring a wide-open field beyond Sinner's form edge.

Santos' mixed recent form (two wins, one draw, two losses in last five) faces challenges from injuries to Rony, Mayke, Gabriel Menino, and Vinicius Lira, prompting improvisation at right-back despite Neymar and Gabigol availability in a projected 4-2-3-1 under Cuca. Coritiba, with one win and four draws lately, misses Keno, Pedro Morisco, and Rodrigo Rodrigues, relying on Pedro Rocha up top amid solid away resilience. Traders eye key matchups, potential clean sheets, and aggregate implications in this knockout tie.

markets.question: Israel x Hezbollah diplomatic meeting by May 31, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a diplomatic meeting between representatives of Israel and Hezbollah by the listed date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A diplomatic meeting refers to a deliberate meeting between representatives of Israel and Hezbollah who are acting in an official capacity and are authorized to engage in negotiation or diplomacy regarding Israel-Hezbollah relations on behalf of their governments/leadership structures. Meetings conducted indirectly, for example, through designated mediators, facilitators, or interlocutors acting with the knowledge and authorization of the relevant governments, will qualify. 

Brief greetings, chance encounters, or talks otherwise not deliberately aimed at diplomacy or negotiation will not count.

The meeting must be in-person (including indirect in-person meetings) and must be publicly acknowledged by either government or reported by a consensus of credible media. Remote meetings, phone calls, or other meetings where the relevant parties are not present will not count.

The resolution sources for this market will be official information from the government of Israel and Hezbollah leadership, and a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: US-brokered direct talks between Israel and Lebanon, the first in over 30 years, commenced on April 14, 2026, in Washington, focusing on Hezbollah disarmament, border security, and restoring the November 2024 ceasefire amid the ongoing 2026 Lebanon war. A 10-day cessation of hostilities began April 16 and was extended multiple times, but recent mutual violations—including Hezbollah drone attacks injuring Israeli soldiers and Israeli strikes on Beirut suburbs as of May 10—have fueled fears of collapse. Hezbollah leaders reject direct diplomacy with Israel, expressing openness only to indirect negotiations via mediators like the US, France, and UNIFIL, with no verified Israel-Hezbollah meetings reported. Upcoming ambassador-level talks in Washington could test de-escalation prospects, though persistent airstrikes and rocket fire sustain trader skepticism on formal diplomatic breakthroughs.

eventMetadata.context_description: Al Ittihad sit sixth in the Saudi Pro League table with 42 points from 26 matches, but their recent form has faltered with just one win in the last five games (1W-2D-2L), exacerbated by the winter departures of stars Karim Benzema to Al Hilal and N'Golo Kanté, leaving gaps in attack and midfield amid fan frustration ahead of a King's Cup semi-final. Al Shabab, 12th on 29 points, host at SHG Arena on April 10 showing sharper scoring edge lately (three wins in five, 12 goals), buoyed by home strength despite mid-table position and forward Mohammed Harbush sidelined long-term with a cruciate injury. Al Ittihad hold a strong head-to-head edge, unbeaten in the last five meetings, setting up a competitive Riyadh clash influenced by rest advantages and tactical adjustments.

markets.question: Will the lowest temperature in London be 6°C on May 12?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the lowest temperature recorded at the London City Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 12 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the lowest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the London City Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/gb/london/EGLC.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

events.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the lowest temperature recorded at the London City Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 12 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the lowest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the London City Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/gb/london/EGLC.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket clusters around 5°C (28.5%) and 6°C (28.0%) for London's lowest temperature on May 12, reflecting the latest Met Office hourly forecast showing a 6°C minimum near dawn amid sunny intervals, light winds (4-9 mph), and low humidity (40-60%) that enable radiative cooling after evening clearing. The 4°C outcome at 21.5% accounts for model ensemble spread, where greater overnight cloud dissipation or calmer winds could enhance cooling, while residual showers or breezes might hold floors higher near 7°C. Recent northerly airflow has delivered chilly nights this week, with bank holiday lows dipping lower in rural spots, but urban heat in London tempers extremes. New Met Office updates expected tonight could shift probabilities as boundary layer conditions evolve.

markets.question: Iran leadership change by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Supreme Leader of Iran, Mojtaba Khamenei, ceases to be the de facto leader of Iran at any point between market creation and the listed date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Mojtaba Khamenei will be considered to no longer be the de facto leader of Iran if he is removed from power, is detained, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from acting as the de facto leader of Iran within this market's timeframe.

An official announcement of Mojtaba Khamenei’s resignation or removal will qualify for a "Yes" resolution regardless of when the announced departure goes into effect.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mojtaba Khamenei succeeded his father, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, as Iran's Supreme Leader following the elder's assassination in Israeli airstrikes on February 28, 2026, amid escalating US-Israel-Iran conflict, but persistent rumors of Mojtaba's severe injuries—including facial burns, leg surgeries requiring a prosthetic, and speech difficulties—have fueled speculation of his incapacity despite no public appearances since his March appointment. Iranian officials, including President Masoud Pezeshkian who confirmed a recent meeting, insist he remains in good health and active behind the scenes via written directives read on state media. The Assembly of Experts holds authority over any further transition, with trader focus on potential escalations in the war or a verifying video statement that could clarify stability or prompt leadership shifts before year-end resolution.

markets.question: Will Renan Santos finish in second place in the first round of the 2026 Brazilian presidential election?
markets.description:
A presidential election is scheduled to take place in Brazil on October 4, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate who receives the second-most valid votes in the first round of this election.

The named candidates will be primarily ranked by the number of valid votes received in the specified election. If two or more candidates are tied on valid votes, ties will be broken by alphabetical order of the candidates' last names. This market will resolve to the candidate that occupies the second-highest finishing position after applying this ranking.

If the result of this election isn't known definitively by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., https://dadosabertos.tse.jus.br/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent polls from early May, including Ideia and Real Time Big Data, show incumbent President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva leading the first round with 40% of valid votes, while Senator Flávio Bolsonaro trails closely at 34-36%, cementing his position as the clear runner-up far ahead of rivals like Ronaldo Caiado (5%) and Romeu Zema (3-4%). This polling consistency, amid Jair Bolsonaro's ineligibility, has driven trader consensus to price Flávio Bolsonaro at 66.5% for second place, reflecting his consolidation of right-wing support. Lula's 19.5% odds imply a modest risk of him slipping to second if Flávio surges, with fragmented fields keeping others under 5%. The race remains tight ahead of the October 4 first round.

markets.question: Total Kills Over/Under 62.5 in Game 2?
markets.description:
In the upcoming match between Nigma Galaxy and REKONIX in the 1win Essence Group B, initially scheduled for May 2 at 2:30PM ET:

This market is about the total kills in Game 2.

This market will resolve to "Over" if the total kills in Game 2 is 63 or more. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under".

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 begins but is not completed, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is remade, resolution will be based on the total kills in the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.dotabuff.com. However, if https://www.dotabuff.com has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.


In Dota 2, a "kill" for markets like team total kills, kill handicap, and interval kills is counted when an enemy hero dies and the game awards a hero kill event to the opposing team's score, which is the number shown on the in-game scoreboard and tracked in scoreboard feeds as team kills. In practical terms, this includes any enemy hero death that increments a team's kill score, regardless of who got the last hit, including situations where the victim dies to creeps, a tower, or neutrals as long as the kill is still credited to the enemy team. It does not include denies, such as killing your own allied units, and it also does not include building or objective destructions like towers, barracks, or Roshan, since those are tracked separately from kills. Assists also do not count as kills, because they are recorded as a separate player stat and multiple players can assist on a single kill.

events.title: Dota 2: Nigma Galaxy vs REKONIX (BO3) - 1win Essence Group B
events.description:
This market refers to the Dota 2 match between Nigma Galaxy and REKONIX in the 1win Essence Group B, initially scheduled for May 2 at 2:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Nigma Galaxy" if Nigma Galaxy win the match against REKONIX.

This market will resolve to "REKONIX" if REKONIX win the match against Nigma Galaxy.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.dotabuff.com. However, if https://www.dotabuff.com has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Nigma Galaxy dominates Group B in the 1win Essence I Tier 2 Dota 2 tournament with a 4-0 record and 8-0 maps through clean sweeps, including a 2-0 group stage win over REKONIX on May 2, fueled by veteran carry No!ob, recent addition Davai on offlane, and coach KuroKy's strategies adapting to the current patch's versatile meta. REKONIX, a rising Indonesian squad with stable roster since 2025 and trial coach MinD_ContRoL since April, sits 2-2 after mixed results against lower seeds, relying on aggressive laning from Jikroy and inYourdreaM but vulnerable to Nigma's macro execution. No roster changes or stand-ins reported; full-strength matchup emphasizes draft flexibility and series momentum in this round-robin BO3.

markets.question: Will "The Great Divide - Noah Kahan" be the Billboard 200 #1 album for the week of May 16?
markets.description:
Billboard updates the Billboard 200 albums chart each Tuesday (with adjusted release schedules on some holiday weeks), reflecting data from the previous week (Friday-Thursday). Each Billboard chart is then dated “Week of (date of the upcoming Saturday)”.

This market will resolve according to the number 1 album on the Billboard 200 chart dated “Week of May 16, 2026”.

This market will resolve as soon as the relevant chart is published. If the Billboard 200 chart for the specified week is not published within 14 calendar days of the expected release date, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be the Billboard 200 chart for the specified week, published on the Billboard website (https://www.billboard.com/charts/billboard-200/) or through other official Billboard channels.

eventMetadata.context_description: Noah Kahan's The Great Divide commands a 99.6% implied probability for another Billboard 200 #1 as tracking wraps for the May 16 chart, driven by HITS Daily Double's final projection of 162,000 equivalent album units—more than 50,000 ahead of runner-up Ella Langley's Dandelion at 103,000. Building on its blockbuster debut of 389,000 units last week (the year's biggest streaming week for a rock album and Noah Kahan's first #1), sustained pure sales (including strong vinyl), and all 21 tracks charting on the Hot 100 have locked in trader consensus amid minimal competition. Upsets from holdovers like Morgan Wallen's I'm the Problem, Ye's Bully, or Chris Brown's BROWN would require improbable late surges in streaming equivalents, but the gap leaves little room for volatility before Tuesday's chart drop.

markets.question: Will Liberal Alliance be part of the next Government of Denmark?
markets.description:
A Parliamentary election is scheduled to be held in Denmark on March 24, 2026.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed political party is included in the first Danish government formed after the 2026 Danish parliamentary election. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market may resolve once the first government is officially confirmed following the appointment of the Prime Minister and ministers by the monarch after the 2026 Danish parliamentary election, with ministers sworn in under non-caretaker circumstances.

A party will only be considered part of the government if it participates in the governing coalition and provides at least one cabinet minister. Parties that merely support the government from outside the cabinet (e.g., through parliamentary support agreements or similar arrangements) without holding a cabinet post will not qualify.

If no government is formed, or the results are not known definitively by January 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve based on a consensus of credible reporting. In case of ambiguity, this market will resolve based on official information from the Government of Denmark.

eventMetadata.context_description: Following the March 24, 2026 snap Folketing election, Denmark faces a hung parliament with the red bloc holding 86 seats, the blue bloc 78, and centrists like the Moderates (14 seats) pivotal for the 90-seat majority needed. Incumbent Social Democrats leader Mette Frederiksen's coalition talks with Green Left and Social Liberals stalled over policy divides, particularly excluding far-left Red-Green Alliance. On May 8, King Frederik X tasked Venstre leader and former Defence Minister Troels Lund Poulsen to lead center-right negotiations, potentially uniting Venstre, Conservatives, Liberal Alliance, and Moderates—echoing the prior SVM coalition. Talks resume May 11 amid urgency for stability in proportional representation system.

markets.question: Will Opendoor Technologies Inc. (OPEN) hit (LOW) $3.50 Week of May 11 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during the week of May 11 2026, any 1-minute candle for Opendoor Technologies Inc. (OPEN) has a final "Low" price equal to or below the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Opendoor Technologies Inc. (OPEN) "Low" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.OPEN%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.OPEN%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily low price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

markets.question: Will the lowest temperature in New York City be 43°F or below on May 10?

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the lowest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the LaGuardia Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/ny/new-york-city/KLGA.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

markets.question: Will Elon Musk post 840-879 tweets in May 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X during the month of May 2026.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

markets.question: Will Trump say "Darth Vader" in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Donald Trump mentions the listed term between May 1, 2026, 12:00 AM ET and May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Plural and possessive forms of the listed term will count toward the resolution of this market regardless of context; however, other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

A ‘mention’ will include any verbal mention which is recorded (audio or video) and publicly accessible.

Written usages of terms (e.g. Truth Social posts, written statements, etc.) will not count toward this market's resolution.

AI-generated audio or video will not count toward this market's resolution.

Videos posted on his social media which were filmed outside this market's time frame will not qualify for a "Yes" resolution.

markets.question: Dota 2: Power Rangers vs MODUS (BO3) - European Pro League Playoffs
markets.description:
This market refers to the Dota 2 Lower bracket semifinal match between Power Rangers and MODUS in the European Pro League Playoffs, initially scheduled for May 11 at 5:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Power Rangers" if Power Rangers win the match against MODUS.

This market will resolve to "MODUS" if MODUS win the match against Power Rangers.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.dotabuff.com. However, if https://www.dotabuff.com has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

events.title: Dota 2: Power Rangers vs MODUS (BO3) - European Pro League Playoffs
events.description:
This market refers to the Dota 2 Lower bracket semifinal match between Power Rangers and MODUS in the European Pro League Playoffs, initially scheduled for May 11 at 5:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Power Rangers" if Power Rangers win the match against MODUS.

This market will resolve to "MODUS" if MODUS win the match against Power Rangers.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.dotabuff.com. However, if https://www.dotabuff.com has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Power Rangers and MODUS, both group stage runners-up in European Pro League Season 37, clash in a pivotal BO3 lower bracket quarterfinal amid double-elimination playoffs. Power Rangers advanced with resilient lower bracket wins, including 2-0 over Team Nemesis on May 9 and a dominant 2-0 shutout of Rune Eaters on May 10 after their upper bracket quarterfinal loss to Team Lynx. MODUS similarly rebounded, sweeping enjoy boys 2-0 in lower round 1 following a 0-2 upper defeat to Rune Eaters. Power Rangers hold a recent head-to-head edge, winning 2-1 against MODUS in DreamLeague qualifiers last month. Stable rosters and strong recent form highlight a competitive matchup, with momentum favoring the team adapting best to current meta shifts.

markets.question: Will Munetaka Murakami hit the most home runs during the 2026 MLB regular season?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the player who records the most home runs during the 2026 Major League Baseball regular season.

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official leader as determined by the rules of the MLB. If multiple leaders are announced then this market will resolve to the player with the higher slugging percentage during the 2026 MLB regular season. If a tie still persists, then this market will resolve in favor of the player with the higher batting average during the 2026 MLB regular season. If a tie still persists, this market will resolve in favor of the player that records more hits during the 2026 MLB regular season. If a tie still persists, this market will resolve to the player whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

If the 2026 MLB regular season is cancelled, postponed after October 15, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no official leader declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from MLB; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Aaron Judge commands 41.5% implied probability as MLB home run leader despite tying Kyle Schwarber at 16 homers apiece after both slugged their 16th on May 10, buoyed by Judge's elite exit velocity, .266 average, 1.026 OPS, and history of 58- and 53-homer seasons in Yankee Stadium's short porch. Munetaka Murakami's blistering rookie start with the White Sox—15 homers including No. 15 on May 8—has traders assigning 21.5% on his NPB-translated power, though strikeouts pose regression risks. Schwarber's 14% reflects his four-game homer streak and Citizens Bank Park boost, while Matt Olson (14 HR, 8.1%) benefits from Atlanta's lineup protection amid a competitive early-season race projecting 60+ pace leaders.

markets.question: Will Zachariah Wooden advance from the CA-07 primary election?
markets.description:
The California primary is currently scheduled to take place on June 2, 2026.

This market will resolve "Yes" if the listed candidate advances from the primary to contest the seat for California's 7th congressional district in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If no nominees are announced by November 3, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official sources, including https://www.sos.ca.gov/.

Any replacement of the nominees before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

markets.question: Nvidia Data Center Revenue above 50B in Q1?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Nvidia’s data center revenue for the first fiscal quarter of 2027, as reported in its official company earnings materials, is above the listed amount. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.
The specified metric will be considered as reported in the company’s official earnings materials. Subsequent revisions will not be considered.

If the specified company’s official earnings materials for the specified quarter are released, and the specified metric is not included, this market will resolve to “No”. 
 
If the specified company does not release quarterly earnings materials for the specified quarter by July 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “No”.

If the specified metric is reported as a range rather than a specific number, the midpoint of the range will be used for resolution of this market. 

The resolution source for this market is Nvidia’s official company earnings materials, including press releases, investor presentations, and regulatory filings. If the specified metric is not reported in these materials, recordings or transcripts of the company’s earnings webcast may also be used.

Note: This market will resolve based on the most numerically precise version of the specified metric reported in the company’s official earnings materials. Only the specified metric will be considered; alternate versions that differ in definition or scope from the specified metric will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: NVIDIA's fiscal first-quarter 2027 earnings on May 20 serve as the pivotal catalyst shaping trader sentiment on data center revenue thresholds, following guidance for $78 billion total revenue—up 77% year-over-year and excluding China compute sales due to U.S. export curbs. The data center segment, comprising over 90% of recent revenue amid explosive AI demand for Hopper and Blackwell GPUs, delivered $62.3 billion in fiscal Q4 2026, up 75% year-over-year. Recent AI infrastructure alliances and a $2.1 billion investment securing $3.4 billion in future IREN data center revenue underscore sustained hyperscaler capex, though supply chain dynamics and Big Tech spending scrutiny loom as key swing factors.

markets.question: Sabres vs. Canadiens: O/U 5.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming NHL game, scheduled for May 12 at 7:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if the Sabres and Canadiens combine to score 6 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 6, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods and shootouts. In the event of a shootout, one goal will be added to the winning team's score for the purpose of resolution.

events.title: Sabres vs. Canadiens
events.description:
In the upcoming NHL game, scheduled for May 12 at 7:00PM ET:
If the Sabres win, the market will resolve to "Sabres".
If the Canadiens win, the market will resolve to "Canadiens".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods and shootouts. In the event of a shootout, one goal will be added to the winning team's score for the purpose of resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Montreal Canadiens seized a 2-1 series lead over the Buffalo Sabres in their Eastern Conference second-round Stanley Cup Playoffs matchup with a commanding 6-2 Game 3 victory Sunday at Bell Centre, where Alex Newhook tallied two goals and four Canadiens notched multi-point games amid heavy physical play and 44 hits from Buffalo. Canadiens forward Cole Caufield continued heating up with key power-play strikes, while Tage Thompson opened scoring for the Sabres early. Momentum favors Montreal heading into Game 4 at home Tuesday, bolstered by raucous crowd support and recent dominance after dropping Game 1 (4-2); Sabres seek bounce-back with Noah Ostlund out (lower body) but Sam Carrick recently returned from injury, as Patrik Laine remains sidelined for Habs (lower body). Trader consensus reflects Canadiens' home-ice edge and form in this tight Atlantic Division rivalry.

markets.question: Gemini 3.2 released by May 22, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Google's Gemini 3.2 model is made available to the general public by the specified date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

Gemini 3.2 refers to a product explicitly named Gemini 3.2, or a variant that is recognized as a direct successor to Gemini 3.1, similar to the progression from Gemini 3 to Gemini 3.1. (e.g., Gemini 3.2 GA, Gemini 3.2/3.3/3.4, etc., in any variant, like Pro/Deep Think/Flash/Flash-lite, would qualify toward a "Yes" resolution to this market).

Specialized models for non-text modalities such as video generation (e.g., Veo), image generation (e.g., Imagen, Nano Banana), music generation (e.g., Lyria), or robotics (e.g., Gemini Robotics) will NOT qualify, even if released under the Gemini 3.2 version number. Products labeled as a GA promotion of an already-existing Preview model (e.g., Gemini 3.1 GA) or a new flagship generation (e.g., Gemini 4) or similar that are not explicitly labeled as described above will NOT qualify.

A qualifying model must be launched and publicly accessible, including via open beta or open rolling waitlist signups. A closed beta or any form of private access will not suffice. The release must be either clearly defined and publicly announced by Google as being accessible to the general public or otherwise made publicly accessible and explicitly labeled within the company's official website. Labeling errors, placeholder text, or version names displayed on the website that do not correspond to a model that is actually accessible to the general public will not qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Google, with additional verification from a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Leaked integrations of Gemini 3.2 Flash in the Gemini iOS app and Google AI Studio—complete with live pricing at $0.25 per million input tokens—have surfaced over the past week, signaling Google's DeepMind team is on the cusp of releasing this next iteration in its large language model family. Early benchmarks on platforms like LM Arena show it rivaling or exceeding Gemini 3.1 Pro in coding tasks, bolstering trader consensus on Google's lead amid intensifying competition from OpenAI's GPT-5.5 previews and Anthropic's Claude 4.x series. Google I/O on May 19 remains the key catalyst for official announcement and public rollout, with no credible reports of rival labs pursuing a "Gemini 3.2" branding.

markets.question: Will "Michael" be the April film with the highest domestic gross on May 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the title of the film released in April 2026 with the highest domestic gross between opening and May 30, 2026 according to "Daily Box Office Performance" figures found on the “Box Office” tab on this movie's The Numbers (https://www.the-numbers.com/) page.

In the event of an exact tie the film that comes first alphabetically will be considered the winner.

If there is no final data available by June 15, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, another credible resolution source will be chosen.

events.title: Highest Domestically Grossing April Film on May 31?
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the title of the film released in April 2026 with the highest domestic gross between opening and May 30, 2026 according to "Daily Box Office Performance" figures found on the “Box Office” tab on this movie's The Numbers (https://www.the-numbers.com/) page.

In the event of an exact tie the film that comes first alphabetically will be considered the winner.

If there is no final data available by June 15, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, another credible resolution source will be chosen.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Super Mario Galaxy Movie commands a commanding 98% implied probability as the highest-grossing April 2026 domestic release through May 31, driven by its record-shattering $131 million opening weekend on April 1 and sustained family audience appeal from Illumination's vibrant animation and Nintendo's powerhouse IP—echoing the 2023 Super Mario Bros. Movie's billion-dollar triumph. Cumulative domestic grosses exceed $412 million by early May, dwarfing runner-up Michael ($240 million, fueled by Jaafar Jackson's praised portrayal but mixed 39% critics score) and negligible earners like Mother Mary or Wasteland Cop. Trader consensus reflects unassailable momentum, with three straight #1 weekends amid strong 88% audience scores. An upset would require unprecedented legs for Michael amid fading theatrical windows or surprise countersuit delays, though Mario's evergreen draw through Memorial Day makes this improbable.

markets.question: Will Google have the #2 AI model at the end of June 2026 (Style Control On)?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the second-highest arena rank (Style Control On) based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on June 30, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Overall" Leaderboard tab at https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with style control on will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies second place under this ranking system.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.
